title: Executive Order 9100—Exemption of August Thomas From Compulsory Retirement for Age
text:
Whereas
, in my judgment, the public interest requires that August Thomas, chief engineer (towboats), Marine Division, Department of Operation and Maintenance, The Panama Canal, who, during the month of July, 1942, will reach the retirement age prescribed for automatic separation from the service, applicable to him, be exempted from compulsory retirement for age for a period of one year:
Now, Therefore
, by virtue of and pursuant to the authority vested in me by section 204 of the act of June 30, 1932, 47 Stat. 382, 404 (U.S.C., title 5, sec. 715a), I hereby exempt the said August Thomas from compulsory retirement for age for a period of one year ending July 31, 1943.
FRANKLIN D ROOSEVELT
The White House,
March 16, 1942.
